title: marsilea exarata
intro: Marsilea exarata, also known as the Australian waterclover, is a type of aquatic fern native to Australia and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Marsilea exarata include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Marsilea exarata can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall well-being.
